# Implementation Readiness Checklist: Update Grayline Terminator

**Purpose**: Validate whether grayline refresh requirements are precise enough to support implementation planning and review without relying on unstated assumptions.
**Created**: 2026-05-18
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are requirements defined for all implementation-relevant refresh entry points: initial map availability, scheduled visible-session refresh, visibility-return refresh, device sleep/resume, and update failure? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-002a, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK002 Are all state-preservation targets listed consistently across requirements, success criteria, and tasks, including map position, zoom, base map, callsign, WSPR data, selected path, open controls, support links, analytics behavior, and version status? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK003 Are desktop and mobile-sized session expectations specified with enough detail to guide acceptance criteria and task coverage? [Gap, Spec §FR-009]
- [x] CHK004 Are requirements defined for the initial overlay state before the first successful grayline calculation completes? [Gap, Spec §US1]
- [x] CHK005 Are requirements defined for repeated grayline refresh failures, not just a single failed update? [Gap, Spec §FR-007]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is the term "immediately" for visibility-return refresh quantified with a maximum acceptable delay or event boundary? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-002a, Spec §SC-005]
- [x] CHK007 Is "without visible interruption" specified with observable criteria such as no focus change, no map reset, and no control dismissal? [Ambiguity, Spec §US2, Spec §SC-004]
- [x] CHK008 Is "subtle" overlay presentation constrained by measurable readability or contrast requirements? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK009 Is "active open-map session" defined in terms of visible, hidden, backgrounded, and resumed page states? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002]
- [x] CHK010 Is the expected behavior for significant device clock changes forward or backward clearly specified? [Clarity, Spec §US3]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Are the cadence requirements consistent between the spec, plan, UI contract, and tasks? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002, Plan §Constraints]
- [x] CHK012 Are visibility-return requirements consistent between the clarification, functional requirements, success criteria, and task descriptions? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §FR-002a, Spec §SC-005]
- [x] CHK013 Are "grayline", "terminator", "day/night terminator", and "day/night boundary" defined as either equivalent or intentionally distinct terms? [Terminology, Spec §Key Entities]
- [x] CHK014 Are no-new-control scope boundaries consistent across the spec, plan, and task phases? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010, Plan §Constraints]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Can the 5-minute cadence requirement be objectively evaluated without requiring a real 30-minute or 2-hour manual wait? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001, Spec §SC-002]
- [x] CHK016 Are acceptance criteria for visibility return separate from scheduled-refresh acceptance criteria? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §US1, Spec §US3]
- [x] CHK017 Are state-preservation acceptance criteria specific enough to determine which controls and overlays must remain unchanged? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-003]
- [x] CHK018 Are the success criteria for user interaction during refresh precise enough to distinguish map interaction, callsign entry, manual WSPR refresh, and control usage? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-004]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK019 Are primary scenarios for visible-session refresh, hidden-tab return, and multi-hour monitoring all independently described? [Coverage, Spec §User Stories]
- [x] CHK020 Are exception scenarios for calculation failure, overlay display failure, and timer/visibility event failure documented or intentionally excluded? [Gap, Spec §FR-007]
- [x] CHK021 Are recovery scenarios defined after a failed refresh, device sleep, and device clock change? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §US3]
- [x] CHK022 Are non-functional scenarios for constrained devices and mobile-sized sessions clear enough to guide review? [Coverage, Spec §FR-009, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK023 Are high-latitude and international date-line requirements included in the feature requirements rather than only implied by implementation tasks? [Gap, Tasks §US1]
- [x] CHK024 Are overnight and multi-hour session requirements aligned so they do not create conflicting freshness expectations? [Consistency, Spec §US1, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK025 Are hidden/backgrounded tab requirements clear when the tab is hidden for less than 5 minutes versus more than 5 minutes? [Ambiguity, Spec §SC-005]
- [x] CHK026 Are requirements defined for preserving selected-path detail state during refresh, if selected-path details are considered part of visible WSPR data? [Gap, Spec §FR-005]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK027 Are responsiveness expectations for grayline refresh quantified beyond broad UX language? [Clarity, Plan §Performance Goals]
- [x] CHK028 Are accessibility requirements specified for ensuring the overlay does not reduce control readability or operability? [Gap, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK029 Are analytics and privacy requirements explicit about whether grayline refreshes should avoid custom tracking events? [Gap, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK030 Are resource-use requirements clear enough to balance refresh frequency against battery/performance impact? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK031 Is the dependency on the user's device clock documented as an explicit assumption with known limitations? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK032 Is the no-new-production-dependency decision traceable from plan assumptions to requirement scope? [Assumption, Plan §Technical Context]
- [x] CHK033 Are existing app dependencies that must remain unaffected, such as support links, analytics, and version status, traceable to success criteria or acceptance scenarios? [Dependency, Spec §FR-006]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK034 Is there any unresolved conflict between conserving resources while hidden and keeping long-running sessions current? [Conflict, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-002a]
- [x] CHK035 Does the spec distinguish calculated grayline accuracy from visual overlay clarity so implementation can prioritize both separately? [Clarity, Spec §Key Entities, Spec §SC-002]
- [x] CHK036 Are task-level expectations for support links, analytics, version status, and mobile viewport coverage consistent with the broader requirements? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-009]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
